fields.description:
## What
An OPTIONAL, isolated backfill adapter that mines this machine's Claude Code session transcripts to reconstruct the LOCAL env/integration-diagnosis history (and corroborate attempts/replanning/recovery), which no structural signal captures. Output is LLM-classified and clearly labeled low-confidence; the core command does not depend on this adapter.

## Files
- `src/rebar/metrics/adapters/__init__.py`, `src/rebar/metrics/adapters/claude_transcripts.py` â€” new isolated adapter (imported only by the backfill script, never by the core command).
- `scripts/backfill_transcripts.py` â€” new: targeted extraction (grep error signatures) -> classify -> write labeled snapshots.
- `tests/unit/metrics/test_transcript_adapter.py` â€” new.

## Approach
Because all development happened on one machine, the transcripts are a near-complete local record of env toil that leaves no structural trace. To keep the harness portable, the adapter is optional and quarantined behind the backfill script â€” a parallel adapter could target another harness. It is a targeted pass: an **error-signature grep** over the transcript JSONL (patterns such as `LLMUnavailableError`, `ConnectionError`/`connection refused`, `ModuleNotFoundError`, `command not found`, `No such file`, HTTP `429`, `timed out`) selects candidate lines, then a **deterministic keyword/regex classifier** (NOT an LLM â€” so the adapter and its test are self-contained and reproducible, with no model call or human-calibration gate) maps each signature to the classification enum. Every emitted value carries `source=backfill_classified, confidence=classified` â€” that label IS the honest low-confidence signal, and it segregates these values from authoritative structural series. The classification enum (`env_setup`, `dependency`, `integration`, `tooling`, `none`) and the JSON output schema are defined in the adapter module.

## Tests
- Unit (fixture JSONL transcripts): a transcript with a known env-failure signature is extracted and classified into an `env_setup` interval; a clean transcript yields none; output carries the classified label.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `tests/unit/metrics/test_transcript_adapter.py` passes: a fixture JSONL transcript containing a known env-failure signature (e.g. a `ModuleNotFoundError` line) is extracted and deterministically classified into at least one interval tagged `source=backfill_classified`, `confidence=classified` (`jq -e`); a clean transcript yields 0. No LLM/network call occurs (the classifier is deterministic).
- [ ] the portability guard holds: a unit test asserts the CORE metrics package does NOT import the adapter â€” specifically that `src/rebar/metrics/registry.py` and `src/rebar/metrics/__init__.py` (the modules that exist today) do not import `adapters.claude_transcripts` (and the future `_commands/metrics.py`, once ticket 9a5a lands it, is held to the same rule). Assert by scanning the module source for the adapter import.
- [ ] `scripts/backfill_transcripts.py` is exercised: a test invokes its entry point over a fixture transcript dir and asserts it emits the labeled classified records (so the script â€” not just the adapter â€” is covered).
- [ ] the classification enum (`env_setup`, `dependency`, `integration`, `tooling`, `none`) and output schema are defined in the adapter module â€” test asserts the enum has exactly these values.
